steps.title: Understanding margin of error
steps.content: Margin of error represents the range around the sample statistic where the true population parameter is estimated to lie. To find this range, we subtract and add the margin of error from the sample mean.

steps.title: Calculate the confidence interval bounds
steps.content:
Lower bound: $10.83 - 0.2 = 10.63$ ounces
Upper bound: $10.83 + 0.2 = 11.03$ ounces
The plausible values for the population average are between 10.63 and 11.03 ounces.

steps.title: Identify the correct interpretation
steps.content:
The answer must describe plausible values between 10.63 and 11.03 ounces.

Choice A states: Plausible values for the average weight of all bags of sugar filled by the machine are between 10.63 ounces and 11.03 ounces. This exactly matches our interval.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
